title: senecio proteus
intro: Senecio proteus, also known as Groundsel or Butterprint, is a plant species in the daisy family with a history of traditional use in herbal medicine. The plant's leaves and flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Senecio proteus include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to support various health conditions, but its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
